{{Quote|Beep boo weep. Twee twee.|A 3C-series utility droid|<ref name="kotor2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>}}
The '''3C-series utility droid''' was a series of [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droid]] manufactured by [[Duwani Mechanical Products/Legends|Duwani Mechanical Products]] prior to the [[Dark Wars]] and in use at the same [[Time/Legends|time]] as the [[T3-series utility droid]]. In the [[3951 BBY|final year]] of the Dark Wars, one 3C-series unit, [[3C-FD]], helped utility [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[T3-M4]] repair the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' on [[Peragus Mining Facility|Peragus]] but was later destroyed by an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|Beep bee. Boop bop beep.|Typical [[Binary/Legends|droid speak]] of 3C-series utility droid|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
The 3C-series [[Cleaning droid/Legends|utility droid]] was manufactured by [[Duwani Mechanical Products/Legends|Duwani Mechanical Products]] sometime during the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the [[Jedi Civil War]] and the [[Dark Wars]].<ref name="kotor2" /> Similar to the other Duwani-made utility [[Droid/Legends|droid]] models of the time, the 3C [[Droid/Legends|droid]] had four wheeled [[Limb/Legends|legs]], of which the front two were attached to the droid's blocky chassis by rotating joints, allowing the droid to slide backward and forward to adjust the unit's height. Including the droid's flat rotating head, the unit stood 0.96 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] high. The droid's equipment included a multi-functional arm, auditory and sonar [[scanner]]s, radar [[Eye/Legends|eye]], and visual [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] capable of handling all base colors.<ref name="TNEGD" />

==History==
Manufactured sometime before [[3951 BBY]],<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> one of these utility droids, [[3C-FD]], was on board the [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|light freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' when the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] escaped from the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-infiltrated [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] [[Harbinger (Hammerhead-class)|''Harbinger'']] in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]]. After their close escape, which resulted in the ''Ebon Hawk'' being badly damaged and drifting in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] close to the [[Peragus Mining Facility]], 3C-FD helped another Duwani-made utility droid, [[T3-M4]], fix the ship. After their repairs, they [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] the ''Ebon Hawk'' to the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] facility, but before they docked the ship, 3C-FD was shot repeatedly and damaged beyond repair by an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]] unit that had been hiding in the damaged vessel.<ref name="kotor2" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Beep boo boo. Doo...|Unhappy 3C-series utility droid|<ref name="kotor2" />}}
The only 3C-series utility droid in [[Star Wars Legends]], 3C-FD, appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', a [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] developed by [[Obsidian Entertainment]]. The 3C-series appears and functions similarly to the [[T3-series utility droid]] and uses a different colored sprite in the game.<ref name="kotor2" /> ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', a [[2006]] reference book written by [[Daniel Wallace]], features an entry for 3C-FD, giving the technical data for this droid series.<ref name="TNEGD" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1st}}
